pattern: Fixing the longest possible prefix equal to the target, then bumping one position up and sorting the rest, yields the smallest permutation that still exceeds the target.
hints: To be smallest yet larger than target, agree with target for as long as you can and exceed it as late as possible.
For each candidate position, you must be able to spend the target's characters on the equal prefix, then place a strictly larger available character there.

Try every position from last to first as the place you first exceed target; using the remaining multiset, put the smallest available character greater than target at that spot and append the leftovers in ascending order, keeping the best overall.

edge_inputs.description: rearrange to just clear the target
no permutation can exceed the target
long equal prefix then a bump

stress.description: small strings over a tiny alphabet so the brute force stays fast and ties abound
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randrange(1, max(2, size) + 1)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(n))
    t = ''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(n))
    return [s, t]
stress.note: small n keeps the exponential oracle quick
